title: woodsia hyperborea subsp. ilvensis
intro: Woodsia hyperborea subsp. ilvensis, also known as Ilvensis Woodsia fern, is a rare and delicate subspecies of the Woodsia hyperborea fern, native to specific regions with moist and humid environments. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Woodsia hyperborea subsp. ilvensis are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of respiratory and skin conditions. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or used in herbal infusions, with the rhizome often being dried and used in various herbal preparations.
